title: Array of events not supported
body:
I am attempting to compile a list of RTL and tb files. When I define an array of events, I encounter no issue:

` event e_all_xfers_completed[4];`

However, once I attempt to use this in a fork as follows:

```
  task run_traffic_per_port(int port_id); 
    fork
      automatic int id = port_id;
      begin
        fork
          begin
            @(e_all_xfers_monitored_tx[id]);
            debug($sformatf("[%t] [Per-Port Guard %0d] TX monitoring complete", $time, id));
          end
          begin
            @(e_all_xfers_monitored_rx[id]);
            debug($sformatf("[%t] [Per-Port Guard %0d] RX monitoring complete", $time, id));
          end
        join  
        // Both complete - signal this port is done
        ->e_all_xfers_completed[id];
        $display("[%t] [Per-Port Guard %0d] All transfers completed", $time, id);
      end
    join_none 
  endtask
```
The output is:

`%Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null;`

When I try to debug, I do not get any more meaningful information (snippet):

`Warning-suppressed-WIDTHTRUNC: verif/src/classes/FLIT_env.sv:136:18: Operator FUNCREF 'generate_packet' expects 2 bits on the Function Argument, but Function Argument's VARREF 'port_id' generates 32 bits....
%Error: export VERILATOR_ROOT=/path/to/verilator
%Error: ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; ` 

I checked the revision history of 5.043, but this is not listed as a "Fix", so I assume the version I am using should be fine.

**What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?**

`verilator --timing --debug --binary -f src_files.f -f sim_files.f --top tb  -Wno-lint  -CFLAGS -std=c++20`

**What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?**
Verilator 5.040
Yes, VCS and Vivado.
**What OS and distribution are you using?**

Rocky Linux release 8.10 (Green Obsidian)
